preamble: The shift from the velar nasal [ŋ] to the voiced velar stop [ɡ] is a process of denasalization and fortition. It involves raising the soft palate to block nasal airflow, converting a nasal sonorant into an oral obstruent while maintaining the velar place of articulation.

examples.note: Initial denasalization is a known tendency in Koreanic phonology where nasals strengthen toward voiced stops, often observed in emphatic or clinical (hyponasal) speech.
Spontaneous denasalization of word-final nasals into voiced stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
